name: Rebekah Rencher
ai_summary: Rebekah Rencher is a 26-year-old from Jonesboro, GA with extensive experience in summer camps and babysitting for children ages 5-9. She is available FT/PT around school hours and approaches conflict resolution by separating children, having one-on-one conversations, and ensuring apologies.

transcripts.q3:
Good afternoon. My name is Rebecca Rancher. I am 26 years old and I am from Atlanta Georgia. I have a lot of experience with working in age, groups between five and nine years old ever since I was about a teenager, I did start babysitting for friends and family. As I got older, I started to volunteer with
 With my mom who is a teacher doing, Vacation, Bible School, and also assisting with her with summer camps that she had through the school system. And then eventually, I began to work at Summer Camps on my own as an adult with the City of College Park. At The Tracey Wyatt Center. A lot of my favorite thing about working with children is just how
 So energetic and creative and wholesome, they can be and I really do enjoy contributing to their development and giving them a happy childhood experience that a lot of kids don't get to have and it just brings me joy knowing that I can make that happen. Thank you.
transcripts.q4: I am located in Jonesboro Georgia. The farthest that I am willing to travel is about 30 miles. The closest major city to me is Atlanta, Georgia, and the surrounding cities are in Fayetteville, Georgia, McDonough, and Mauro. Those are the bigger cities that surround the location that I am in.
transcripts.q5: I am looking for part-time work. I do have availability in the morning and the afternoon so I am open to do daytime and after school activities as well, I don't mind being available for full-time work if it is needed but my main desire is to be a full-time worker.
transcripts.q6:
The first thing that I would do is separate the two children and make sure that they, it is not possible for the two to conflict with each other. In that moment, following that I will make sure that I have a one-on-one conversation with each child just to determine what happened and then have a follow-up conversation with how we will resolve this the next time. So these issues will not happen again.
 And in the end, I will make sure that the two children will apologize to each other. Because regardless of who made what mistake we do? Need to know that it did, we have to forgive each other so we can move on and both have a great day and we don't have the same issues again.
transcripts.q7: I think I would make a great, a kid teacher because I am very understanding and very kind and I just bring a gray ray of sunshine. Wherever I go, I love being able to interact with people and get to know people and I think I would contribute very well to what a lot of daycares are looking for.
